fundamentals.name: Netflix, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Netflix, Inc. provides entertainment services worldwide. The company offers television (TV) series, documentaries, feature films, games, and live programming across various genres and languages. It also provides members the ability to receive streaming content through a host of internet-connected devices, including TVs, digital video players, TV set-top boxes, and mobile devices. Netflix, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Los Gatos, California.

judgment.summary: Netflix demonstrates robust growth in revenue and earnings, supported by strong margins and significant free cash flow, despite a moderate debt load.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 1.4%; next earnings date is 2026-07-16.
positives: Revenue growth 16.2% YoY and earnings growth 86.4% YoY indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Operating margin of 32.3% and profit margin of 28.5% show excellent profitability.
Return on equity at 48.5% highlights efficient use of shareholder capital.
Free cash flow of $25.99B TTM provides substantial financial flexibility.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target of $114.56.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 53.79% indicates a notable level of leverage.
PEG ratio of 1.74 suggests the stock may be somewhat richly valued relative to expected earnings growth.
Last quarter's EPS slightly missed estimates, though the surprise was minimal.

news.paragraph: Netflix is being discussed in the context of its competitive moat in the streaming industry, with recent articles exploring its market position. While no direct company news was released in the past few days, the ongoing industry analysis and analyst commentary suggest continued focus on its subscriber growth and content strategy. The upcoming earnings report on July 16th will be a key event to watch for any shifts in performance or outlook.
